notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET (cell is frozen, daemon=incapable). kiro streams a longer assistant reply but the on-disk transcript records it as ONE whole AssistantMessage line appended after generation (FINDINGS.md §5) — incremental streaming is TUI-render only and never reaches the JSONL the daemon tails, so the streaming-window no-flicker invariant has NO partial-flush trace to assert over and no live recording is taken. This spec is retained as the no-flicker ideal a recording WOULD validate should kiro switch to incremental rollout writes: one contiguous working→ready arc across the streamed turn, no premature flicker to ready before the turn-end signal, no waiting. The kiro parser (parser.go:45-83) maps a text-only AssistantMessage to turn_done (working→ready) and an AssistantMessage with toolUse to assistant (mid-turn, sustains working); there is no assistant_streaming / non-terminal stop_reason family, so IF partial flushes existed they would sustain working correctly — but kiro's transport never produces them. The torn/partial-trailing-LINE robustness the scenario name evokes is a shared-tailer property (tailer.go errPartialAtEOF) covered by synthetic unit tests (large_line_test.go), not a kiro-recordable behavior; a live CLI cannot be forced to emit a mid-line flush on demand (that lives in the separate oversized-transcript-line cell). Recorded via the INTERACTIVE kiro driver (drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh tmux REPL) — headless `kiro-cli chat --no-interactive` writes NO session file and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7); the REPL keeps kiro alive across the send + wait_turn so the working→ready settle is observed before a single tmux teardown yields one clean process_exited. SESSION IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript materializes), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session; turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working (necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working), and every later phase pins to it via same_session_as. OBSERVATIONS: kiro's JSONL carries no model/token/cost (FINDINGS.md §4) — those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar the parser does not read — so model is unknown and cost/tokens are zero. HOW runs differ: exact poem wording, sub-second timing, and credits vary (model-output variance); the state topology (single sustained working span, no waiting, one process_exited, final ready) is stable. Single UUID rollout, one process_exited.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the UUID-keyed rollout transcript has not materialized yet)

User prompt for a longer multi-line reply → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working). PLAIN ANCHOR for the first working in the recording — the no-flicker/no-waiting invariants live on the bounded streaming_window phase below so an unbounded invariant here can't catch the legitimate end-of-turn working→ready

During the streamed reply the session stays solidly in working — no partial flush may promote to ready and state never enters waiting. (DOCUMENTED TARGET: kiro's transport lands the whole AssistantMessage atomically, so today there is no partial-flush window; this asserts the no-flicker ideal a recording would validate if kiro streamed to the JSONL.)
Only the terminal text-only AssistantMessage settles working→ready; despite the streamed reply the turn is one contiguous arc and state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session (DiscoverPIDByCWD on the kiro-cli parent whose OS cwd == session cwd, FINDINGS.md §1)
REPL torn down once after the streamed turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
